turns.user_message: There’s this heavy, cold weight sitting right in my stomach that just won’t budge. It started creeping in a few weeks ago and now it feels like a permanent resident. I keep replaying every little mistake I made lately, and it’s exhausting to carry around. I try to brush it off like it’s nothing, but the guilt just sits there waiting for me to notice it again. I guess I’m just tired of feeling like I’m constantly falling short of my own basic expectations.

That same heavy, cold weight in my stomach has completely taken over my daily routine. It’s not just about the little things anymore, because I’m starting to notice a pattern where I always end up disappointing myself. I catch myself holding back from trying new things because I already know I’ll just mess it up and feel worse. The shame builds up quietly until it feels like I’m walking around with a giant sign pointing out every single flaw. I just want to shake it off, but it keeps anchoring me to the same old regrets.

That heavy, cold weight in my stomach has finally settled into my bones and I don’t even know how to pretend it’s going away anymore. Every choice I make feels tainted by this deep, quiet shame that tells me I’m fundamentally flawed at my core. I keep looking back at everything I’ve touched and only seeing a long trail of things I ruined or handled poorly. It’s exhausting to carry around this constant proof that I’m just not good enough, no matter how hard I try to fix myself. I’m so tired of being the common denominator in every single disappointment.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This has been going on for a while.
